key_insight: The Large Cancer Assistant (LCA) is a model-agnostic orchestration framework that separates multimodal oncology data routing from AI inference to make clinical decision support more modular and scalable.
clinical_relevance: If validated further, this design could help hospitals integrate different oncology AI models more flexibly without tightly coupling them to local IT systems.
method: Formalizes LCA as a 7-tuple architecture built on Algorithmic Impermeability.
Uses an Entry Theory with Geometric Deep Learning to standardize multimodal patient data across structural and medical dimensions.
Routes data through a Cancer Switching Module and outputs a Standardized Intermediate Payload (SIP) for downstream AI execution.
evidence_signal: In a proof-of-concept, the framework was tested across four technical scenarios and showed negligible orchestration overhead, invariant routing during model swaps, and 100% recall for targeted supplementary data requests under injected anomalies.
watch_item: The findings come from a technical proof-of-concept, so real-world clinical utility, workflow integration, and robustness across diverse hospital settings still need validation.
paper_title: The Large Cancer Assistant (LCA): A Model-Agnostic Orchestration Framework for Scalable Clinical Decision Support in Oncology
